reasoning_quote: Baseline zero-init MLP + polynomial STLSQ establishes the first valid reference; step forcing is the dominant rollout failure, so future work should target discontinuous-input generalization and physically structured friction terms.
notes: arch=mlp, lib=polynomial, opt=stlsq, degree=2, threshold=0.025, deriv=autograd, time_fourier=False
conclusion: Kept as baseline because it is the only completed run so far and gives finite metrics across all forcing families. The model is over-complex for a physical law and performs worst on step forcing, indicating the next experiment should improve input-discontinuity handling or add nonlinear friction/cubic spring features.

reasoning_quote: Degree-3 relative-coordinate SINDy with exact kinematics over-expanded the library; validation overflowed and the learned rollout diverged, so the idea is rejected despite physically cleaner position rows.
notes: arch=mlp, lib=polynomial, opt=stlsq, degree=3, threshold=0.05, deriv=autograd, time_fourier=False
conclusion: Discarded. The candidate exposed useful relative coordinates and enforced dx/dt rows, but the cubic cross-term library was too ill-conditioned for the autograd smoother derivatives and produced an unstable vector field with BAD_SCORE.

config_summary: architecture=mlp; hidden_dim=128; num_layers=4; activation=tanh; use_time_fourier=False; batch_size=1024; w_kinematic=0.05; w_smooth=0.0001; derivative_method=autograd; sindy_library=polynomial; sindy_degree=2; sindy_use_relative_features=True; sindy_optimizer=stlsq; sindy_threshold=0.035; sindy_alpha=1e-05; post_enforce_kinematics=True
reasoning_quote: Degree-2 relative-coordinate SINDy with exact kinematics improved score and reduced complexity versus baseline; step forcing worsened, but the lower average rollout and simpler model make it the new benchmark.
notes: arch=mlp, lib=polynomial, opt=stlsq, degree=2, threshold=0.035, deriv=autograd, time_fourier=False
conclusion: Kept as new best. Relative coordinates and exact kinematic rows reduced complexity from 84 to 67 and lowered val_score from 1.442373370 to 1.401344734. The regression remains weak on step forcing, so the next loop should target discontinuous-input robustness without expanding to unstable cubic cross terms.

reasoning_quote: Polynomial plus unary friction terms produced a dense high-gain vector field and diverged in validation; direct tanh/drag features are too ill-conditioned with the current smoother derivatives.
notes: arch=mlp, lib=poly_friction, opt=stlsq, degree=2, threshold=0.04, deriv=autograd, time_fourier=False
conclusion: Discarded. The feature idea is physically motivated, but adding tanh, quadratic drag, and unary cubic terms to every coordinate overfits the smoother derivatives and creates unstable validation rollouts. Keep the degree-2 relative-coordinate model as the benchmark.
